parsed_review.summary: CP00-588 is the sixth descriptor-only pack in the RP19 Client Portal series (CP583-588), covering 10 units RP19.P03.M05.S07-S16 (Permission And Audit Binding), risk class A. The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly false (runtime_ready false); the production_ready_flag 'client_portal_core_p03_permission_slice_descriptor_verified' is a descriptor-verification label only and does not weaken any runtime gate. Scope binding is consistent across manifest, review-baseline, closeout-pack-plan.json (packs[CP00-588]), registry.js CP588 binding, contract current_pack/historical_pack_bindings (tail = CP00-588), and model.test.js, with deliverable distribution {contract:2,implementation:3,test:3,hermes_evidence:1,claude_review:1} and micro-title Permission And Audit Binding x10 all matching with no drift. Descriptor/coverage validators (validateClientPortalCoreCp588Coverage, validateClientPortalCoreCp588P03PermissionSliceDescriptor) enforce descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false/customer_safe/raw_payload=false per row and authority boundaries. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: freezeCp588Result and contract p03_permission_slice_descriptor set writes_product_state, evaluates_runtime_permission, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, creates/updates_database_rows, reads/writes_object_storage all false with synthetic_only/no_real_data true; a grep for write/network/db/child_process/runtime-import APIs in packages/client-portal/src returned no matches. Claude remains read_only and explicitly not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false everywhere). Hermes H19 patterns are used solely for deterministic descriptor evidence packets/gates, not embedded product runtime (hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). CP178+ hardening: readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1456-byte well-formed JSON stdout, not 0-byte/malformed); post-review artifacts are correctly absent as outputs of this review step. command-evidence.json records all 27 implementation/plan/repo-wide validation commands at exit 0. No P0 or P1 issues; one non-blocking P3 observation. Pack is ready to proceed to the gated post-review closeout sequence under human final approval.

findings.title: P03.M05 descriptor rows carry only base flags (no ROW_EXTRAS semantic descriptors)
findings.details: CLIENT_PORTAL_CORE_CP588_ROW_EXTRAS inherits the shared ROW_EXTRAS table, but the ten P03.M05 row titles for this pack (pagination_or_filtering_contract, serialization_guard, unauthorized_data_omission, api_fixture, contract_test, invalid_request_test, denied_response_test, hermes_api_evidence, claude_interface_prompt, documentation_example) have no matching keys in that table. As a result each CP588 row resolves to the six base descriptor properties only (title, descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false, section_micro_phase_id) without title-specific semantic annotations such as the deny-over-allow / unauthorized-data flags present on earlier-phase rows. This is consistent with the generator pattern (ROW_EXTRAS is supplemental and keyed to P00/P01 titles) and is fully accepted by validateClientPortalCoreCp588P03PermissionSliceDescriptor, which only requires the base flags. No-leak guarantees are enforced at the result-wrapper level (freezeCp588Result sets omits_unauthorized_data:true and exposes_*:false) and via required_no_leak_guards, so the thin row descriptors do not weaken any boundary.
findings.recommendation: No action required for closeout. Optionally, if richer per-row semantics are desired for the API-contract phase (P03.Mxx) rows in future packs, extend the shared ROW_EXTRAS map with keys for these titles. Treat as informational only.
